race.name: Lake Placid Loppet
race.tagline: Fifty kilometers over Olympic-legacy trails — where 1,120 meters of Adirondack climbing separates skiers from survivors.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple aid stations along the course

climate.description: Lake Placid's mountain setting delivers cold, snowy winters. January temperatures range from -20°C to -5°C. The Adirondacks receive heavy snowfall, and the Olympic venue's elevation ensures reliable natural coverage. Wind exposure at higher elevations adds a chill factor.
challenges: Bitter Adirondack cold, especially at higher elevations on the course
Significant climbing means wide effort swings and sweat management
Snow conditions vary with altitude — icy at top, softer in valleys

course.primary: The most vertically challenging marathon ski race in the eastern US. The course traverses Olympic-era trails around Lake Placid with 1,120 meters of cumulative climbing through dense Adirondack forest, punishing uphills, and technical descents.
course.surface: Machine-groomed Olympic legacy trails through Adirondack forest
course.grooming: Olympic venue standard grooming maintained by the ORDA trail crew
features: 1,120m of climbing — the hilliest marathon ski in the East
Technical descents through dense Adirondack forest
Panoramic views of the High Peaks from ridgeline sections
Classic Olympic village start and finish atmosphere

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Olympic legacy trails and dramatic Adirondack scenery deliver a uniquely challenging American loppet. The 1,120m of climbing is exceptional for the eastern US. Limited field size keeps it from higher tiers.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: TIER 2 — Olympic pedigree and mountain terrain lift it above most US marathons

history.summary: Founded around 1982, the Lake Placid Loppet capitalizes on the cross-country ski infrastructure built for the 1980 Winter Olympics. The race traverses trails that once hosted Olympic competition, making it one of the few American citizen races on genuine Olympic-legacy terrain. With over 1,100 meters of climbing, it is widely regarded as the most physically demanding marathon ski race in the eastern United States.
notable_facts: Uses trails built for the 1980 Lake Placid Winter Olympics
1,120m of climbing makes it the hilliest eastern US ski marathon
Lake Placid is one of only three US cities to host the Winter Olympics (1932, 1980)
The Olympic Regional Development Authority (ORDA) maintains the trail system year-round
Attracts serious endurance skiers seeking mountainous terrain rarely found east of the Rockies
Multiple technique options — classic, freestyle, or any — reflect its inclusive ethos
